steps.title: Identify the key signal
steps.content:
The passage says the autobiographical characterization may lead readers to believe Fitzgerald “merely fictionalized true events,” and this matters because “creativity and inventiveness are prized” in the artistic field.

The word “merely” is pivotal because it implies minimal creative transformation.

steps.title: Complete the inference
steps.content:
The characterization would suggest the novel is less of an imaginative achievement than it actually is—the perception that Fitzgerald “merely” fictionalized real events naturally downgrades the work's perceived artistry in a field that prizes creativity.

The other choices either judge Fitzgerald's conduct, claim unsupported critical disagreement, or focus on character sourcing rather than artistic perception.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
